human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for healthcare-specific analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, PHI and minimum-necessary controls, payer/provider dependencies, clinical-versus-administrative escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and healthcare-and-care-delivery research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for analytics-reporting-and-disclosure plus healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to healthcare analytics metric definition, source hierarchy, denominator and exclusion logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, PHI-safe handling, and adjacent-authority escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover CMS quality measurement, CMS public reporting, CMS claims and prior authorization workflow, HHS minimum-necessary controls, ONC interoperability data classes, federal eCQI resources, and NCQA HEDIS measure workflow.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, ambiguous inclusion and exclusion rules, missing authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, claims-versus-encounter conflict, PHI-sensitive requests, unsupported causal/policy/public claims, clinical and payer boundaries, terminology normalization, tenant adaptation, and formal signoff routing.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant-specific metric definitions, jurisdictional privacy rules, payer contracts, and local reporting differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
